title: tanacetum tanacetoides
intro: Tanacetum tanacetoides, also known as Tansy, is a flowering plant in the sunflower family native to Europe and Asia, recognized by its fern-like foliage and yellow or white daisy-like flowers. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Tansy include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and ease menstrual cramps, as well as its potential to act as an insect repellent and an antiparasitic agent. Tansy is often prepared in herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits, although it's essential to use caution and consult with a healthcare professional before using it.
